spoken: The default screen is a dashboard showing an overview and quick links to the detailed views for each kind of data.
You can navigate the tabs with the mouse or keyboard shortcuts.
G T for Transfers; G, R for the Review Queue; G, E for Settlement.
Ctrl K for quick transaction lookup.
This is the simulated deposit workflow.
NIX
NIX
NIX
NIX
This deposit passed all checks and the investor's account has been credited.
The detail view shows the result of each rule check.
NIX.
The vendor stub uses real Claude Haiku for IQA and OCR. Not production grade, but functional.
The amount mismatch is detected and Flagged for review.
The review queue shows every deposit waiting for an operator decision.
In the review detail, the operator sees the full transfer information and the check images together.
The operator can compare the front and back images directly against what the vendor reported.
NIX
Every action creates an audit trail.
The operator approves the deposit with a note.
Upon approval, The deposit clears and the investor's account is credited immediately.
Settlement packages all cleared deposits into an X9 file - we use a library to generate real ones.
NIX.
Settlement file generated and ready for transmission to the clearing network.
Acknowledge simulates that the clearing bank has received and accepted the settlement file.
Settlement complete. All deposits in this batch are now fully settled.
Let's bounce a check.
Type the first few characters of a transfer ID and press Tab to auto-complete it.
Simulating a reveral due to nonsufficient funds.
The system reverses the original deposit and applies the fee.
Return processed.
The funds are transferred from the investor's account to the fee account.
